=C2=A0

 [ https://substack.com/redirect/719b9c2f-716f-4cd5-9846-19b11b86d234?j=3De=
yJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ] to learn mo=
re about Tegus.
Hi friends =F0=9F=91=8B,
Happy Tuesday! Hope you=E2=80=99re all enjoying the heat, outside and onlin=
e.=C2=A0
I=E2=80=99ve written a bunch about Twitter over the years. Maybe because it=
=E2=80=99s where I met so many of you, maybe it=E2=80=99s because I=E2=80=
=99m too terminally online, or maybe it=E2=80=99s because I=E2=80=99m an ad=
dict. As much as I want to stop, Twitter keeps pulling me back in.=C2=A0
According to some smart people, Twitter is dying. The experience isn=E2=80=
=99t fun anymore. Zuck entered the ring. Elon is messing up. Whatever the e=
xplanation, it feels like things aren=E2=80=99t going as planned at Twitter=
 HQ.=C2=A0
Or are they? Of all of the billions of takes I=E2=80=99ve seen on Twitter, =
there=E2=80=99s one case I haven=E2=80=99t seen made: that Elon, in his ben=
eficence, is killing Twitter on purpose to free us from its shackles.=C2=A0
Is this what I really think is happening? No. I=E2=80=99m not even convince=
d Twitter is dead yet.=C2=A0
My personal view on Elon, not that it matters, is that he=E2=80=99s the bes=
t atoms entrepreneur of our generation [ https://substack.com/redirect/4050=
5142-8f43-4c43-978a-301d0e269481?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZj=
mlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ], that Tesla and SpaceX are unequivocally good th=
ings for humanity, and that very few people besides Elon, if any, could hav=
e pulled off what he did. But running a social network is a very different =
beast =E2=80=93 people don=E2=80=99t bend as predictably as metals. Someone=
 can be world-class at some things and not as good at other things, particu=
larly when they analogize from experiences that aren=E2=80=99t exactly anal=
ogous.=C2=A0
But that=E2=80=99s not fun. That=E2=80=99s not going to get the clicks. If =
I=E2=80=99ve learned anything on Twitter, it=E2=80=99s that you need to tak=
e a stand, and you need to defend it no matter the facts on the ground.=C2=
=A0
So for today, I=E2=80=99m going to LARP as one of those weird nerds who def=
ends Elon=E2=80=99s every move.=C2=A0
Let=E2=80=99s get to it.
Praise Elon
Whitepill: Elon Musk is intentionally killing Twitter for the good of human=
ity. =C2=A0
Elon Musk is the richest person in the world [ https://substack.com/redirec=
t/ede38fda-cebc-4d48-a4ba-4fa5e40429a8?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_Zl=
NuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ] by $18 billion. He=E2=80=99s a member of t=
he PayPal Mafia.=C2=A0 He made electric cars happen. He made reusable rocke=
ts happen. He=E2=80=99s going to hook our brains into the Matrix. He said t=
hat AI was scary before everyone else said that AI was scary. He=E2=80=99s =
single-handedly solving the population crisis. He thinks about everything f=
rom First Principles=E2=84=A2=EF=B8=8F.
You think that Elon Musk couldn=E2=80=99t figure out how to save a silly li=

osition: relative;"><strong>Why Would Elon Want to Kill Twitter?</strong></=
h3><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: =
0 0 20px 0;">Everything Elon does is in service of increasing humanity&#821=
7;s chances of a good future, and Twitter is hurting our chances of a good =
future. </p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: =
26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">It’s a time suck, a focus killer, a rage g=
enerator. It’s always been those things; the good just outweighed the=
 bad if you played it right. That’s increasingly no longer the case.&=
#160;</p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; ma=
rgin: 0 0 20px 0;">It’s fun to get mad at Elon, but let’s be ho=
nest, Twitter was going downhill before Elon. It peaked during lockdowns, a=
nd it’s gotten straight up worse since. </p><p style=3D"color: #=
404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>My b=
est theory for why is that </span><strong>the Rules of the Game became too =
legible</strong><span>. </span></p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-si=
ze: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>Write threads, and =
tell people to like and RT the first tweet once they get to the end. Share =
videos, not links. Talk about how bad things are now and how good they used=
 to be. Dunk on people with big followings. Write the snarkiest possible re=
plies. Get rewarded with likes, RTs, and followers, turn </span><em>those <=
/em><span>into online courses or affiliate links or whatever. </span><=
/p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: =
0 0 20px 0;">Everything seems to grip our attention although we’re no=
t particularly interested in any of it. </p><p style=3D"color: #404040=
; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">To be sure, the =
new For You feed and updated algorithms have made the problem worse, but th=
ey’re just accelerants. The people who’ve chosen to bend over f=
or the algorithm are being rewarded and it’s made the experience wors=
e for everyone else. It’s made us a little stupider as a species.&#16=
0;</p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margi=
n: 0 0 20px 0;">One of people’s more grounded worries about large lan=
guage models (LLMs) is that it’s going to make online content incredi=
bly mid by reading everything on the internet and spitting back something t=
hat looks roughly like the average of everyone's thoughts. A smoothing and =
dulling. Meanwhile, on Twitter, people are already doing that to themselves=
: molding their content to please the algorithm. </p><p style=3D"color=
: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>I=
n the same way that SEO dulled the internet by nudging people to make websi=
tes more formulaic and less creative, and made it worse by serving people t=
he most SEO-tuned content instead of the </span><em>best</em><span> content=
, Twitter’s algorithm dulled us, or at least our characters in the Gr=
eat Online Game, by nudging people to be more formulaic and less creative, =
and made us worse by serving us the most algorithm-tuned content instead of=
 the </span><em>best</em><span> content. </span></p><p style=3D"color:=
 #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Obviousl=
y, tweets that make people angry get more engagement, which probably isn&#8=
217;t healthy, but the more subtly destructive aspect is just that we pour =
the contents of our brains into molds that fit the algorithm’s desire=
s, and lose some of our novelty in the process. </p><p style=3D"color:=
 #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">And we w=
ould have hobbled along down this path, slowly, for years or even decades. =
Wei points out that inertia and “network effects can keep the party g=
oing long after last call.” Twitter has been hilariously poorly run f=
rom the business perspective, but it’s also been maddeningly hard to =
displace. Network effects and accumulated status and curated interest graph=
s are hard things to overcome. </p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-si=

t: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">But Elon has been living the hell of running =

ral dick measuring contest.” </span></p><div class=3D"captioned-=

: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">You think Zuck is going to =

ey’re teaming up to kill Twitter. That’s the real Jiu Jitsu.</p=
><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 =
0 20px 0;">And just like that, Elon accomplished another mission, barely br=
eaking a sweat, in no time at all.</p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size=
: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>Elon officially acqui=
red Twitter on October 27, 2022. In just three quarters, he’s managed=
 to decimate the product, push away advertisers, repel power users, revital=
ize the social network entrepreneurial community, and goad Mark Zuckerberg =
himself into building a competitor. </span><em>In just three quarters</em><=
span>. </span></p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-he=
ight: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>All because he realized before the r=
est of us that Twitter was diseased, and the disease was spreading to all o=
f us, but it wasn’t </span><em>quite</em><span> diseased enough to di=
e on its own. He sacrificed his time and reputation to kill it for the bene=
fit of us all, to increase our chance of a good future. Praise Elon. <=
/span></p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; m=
argin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>Anyway, </span><em>I’m</em><span> not sayin=
g that’s what Elon was thinking, but I’m saying that’s wh=
at I </span><em>would be saying</em><span> if I were one of those weirdos w=
ho defended Elon’s every move. </span></p><h3 class=3D"header-wi=
th-anchor-widget" style=3D"-moz-appearance: optimizelegibility; -moz-osx-fo=

eight: 1.16em; margin: 1em 0 0.625em 0; position: relative;"><strong>Why It=
’s Probably Good That Twitter Is Dying, Whether Elon Meant to Kill It=
 Or Not</strong></h3><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-heig=
ht: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Intentional or not, though, who cares? The e=
nd result is … good. </p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: =
16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">I say this as someone with a =
crippling Twitter addiction, a large following on Twitter, and practically =
no following on Instagram or Threads who built a business on the back of Tw=
itter distribution. It would be great for me if we got 2020 Twitter back. I=
 wish Twitter were still the place that it was, but it’s not. </=
p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0=
 0 20px 0;">Despite my personal interests, I think Twitter’s demise i=
s good on a bunch of different levels. </p><p style=3D"color: #404040;=
 font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>At the most=
 basic, </span><strong>it’s probably healthy for us all to spend less=
 time on Twitter.</strong><span> </span></p><p style=3D"color: #404040=
; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Of course, Twitt=
er can be a great way to discover new information, make friends, find oppor=
tunities, and understand diverse viewpoints. But it feels more difficult to=
 get those benefits today than it used to, probably because the current par=
adigm is played out and has reached the natural endpoint of legible algorit=
hmic feeds. Noise outweighs signal to such an extent that there are more nu=
tritious ways to gather information and people. </p><p style=3D"color:=
 #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Speaking=
 personally, my brain feels more scattered and my focus less focused when I=
 spend more time on Twitter. When I deleted the Twitter app from my phone f=
or a few weeks, my mind felt noticeably cleaner, even if I checked Twitter =
on my laptop and occasionally on my mobile browser. </p><p style=3D"co=
lor: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Star=
ting with questions I want to answer then reading books, reading Substacks,=
 going down rabbit holes with ChatGPT on specific topics, and having good o=
ld fashioned conversations with people tends to be a lot more fruitful.&#16=
0;</p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margi=
n: 0 0 20px 0;">Without Twitter pulling our attention away every few minute=
s, we should be able to get more deep work done, come up with fresher ideas=
, and have the time and space to implement them more completely. I, for one=
, can’t wait to see how productive Elon himself is when he can’=
t Tweet anymore. Fusion? Longevity? Mars? All on the table. </p><p sty=
le=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px =
0;"><span>One level deeper, </span><strong>we need new forms of idea-based =
social networks</strong><span>. </span></p><p style=3D"color: #404040;=
 font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Just because Twit=
ter may be dying does not mean that we won’t need ways to connect onl=
ine. </p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26p=
x; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>The consumer internet really started to take =
off in the late 90s. Twitter was founded in 2006. None of this stuff is </s=
pan><em>that </em><span>Lindy, and I find it very hard to believe that we&#=
8217;ve already arrived at the ideal way to exchange information and ideas =
across billions of connected people. WE ARE STILL SO EARLY. </span></p=
><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 =
0 20px 0;"><span>With Twitter sucking less of the oxygen out of the room, t=
here should be an opportunity for more startups with novel approaches to ca=
pture peoples’ attention and turn it into something productive or at =
least fun. </span><a href=3D"https://substack.com/redirect/c0c83c7b-cdec-4a=
1c-9933-7f76b70ff542?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa=
8-6m8ExkRNpU" rel style=3D"color: #404040; text-decoration: underline;">Far=
caster</a><span>, </span><a href=3D"https://substack.com/notes" rel style=
=3D"color: #404040; text-decoration: underline;">Notes</a><span>, </span><a=
 href=3D"https://substack.com/redirect/3b416dcd-7f3b-4cd2-92a7-cd9e951837e6=
?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU" rel st=
yle=3D"color: #404040; text-decoration: underline;">Subconscious</a><span>,=
 even weirder stuff. Small, cozy group conversations. Shared canvases. Brin=
g it all on.</span></p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-he=
ight: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">I hope and expect that we’ll see a w=
ide array of text-based attempts to get people to think together – pr=
oducts that start with a question and reward people for contributing to an =
answer, products that encourage well-researched debate on hard topics, prod=
ucts designed from the ground up for a world in which AI exists and can hel=
p but in which human connection is still crucial. Maybe we’ll even re=
allocate some of our Twitter time to touching grass together, in-person.</p=
><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 =
0 20px 0;"><span>And then there’s </span><strong>Threads, and Meta mo=
re broadly</strong><span>.</span></p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size:=
 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Threads hit the 100 million =
user mark in under five days, shattering ChatGPT’s recent record. Tha=
t’s incredibly impressive. The product doesn’t feel like it&#82=
17;s quite there yet for me, but part of that might be the dread at trying =
to rebuild a social graph from scratch after years of focusing on Twitter a=
nd ignoring Instagram. </p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px=
; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>In its current form, I don&=
#8217;t think Threads will replace Twitter – it rewards the same stuf=
f that Twitter does, and as Wei wrote: “</span><strong>To succeed at =
carving out unique space in the market, social networks offer their own uni=
que form of status token, earned through some distinctive proof of work.</s=
trong><span>” There’s nothing distinctive about what it takes t=
o get big on Threads besides having a large existing Instagram following.&#=
160;</span></p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26=
px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">But it’s early, and Adam Mosseri and team ke=
ep saying that they’ll continue to evolve the product. I wouldn&#8217=
;t be surprised if they figured it out. It’s hard to bet against Zuck=
=2E (If he wants to go for the jugular,=
 he should acquire Substack, btw.)</p>=
<p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0=
 20px 0;">That said, I don’t think replacing Twitter is the end game =
for Meta. What if Meta is going after a larger prize? </p><p style=3D"=
color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><s=
pan>If I were building the </span><strong>open, social metaverse</strong><s=
pan> in a battle with Apple’s closed, personal one, I’d want to=
 make sure that my user’s social graphs reflected the people they act=
ually wanted to spend time with. Facebook and Instagram’s graphs are =
stale. Maybe Threads is a way to get people to refresh their interests and =
connections so they feel more at-home in the metaverse.</span></p><p style=
=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;=
"><span>If I were building an </span><strong>open-source foundational LLM</=

 Threads to serve you more relevant ads across its Family of Apps. </s=
pan></p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; mar=
gin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>Either way, Meta has shown a willingness to be a mo=

n alternatives to OpenAI in AI, and certainly to Apple in AR/VR/MR, and Twi=
tter fumbling the bag might contribute to that positivity in its own indire=
ct way. </span></p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-h=
eight: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Taken together, Twitter’s loss is o=

 up with novel evolutions of text- and ideas-based social products. And Met=
a might even get the fodder it needs to make big tech more open. </p><=

-weight: bold; line-height: 1.16em; margin: 1em 0 0.625em 0; position: rela=
tive;"><strong>Post-Twitter</strong></h3><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-s=
ize: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">It’s so early in t=
he history of the internet that the odds were always stacked against the fi=
rst generation of social products – Twitter, Facebook, Instagram, Red=
dit, and the like – were going to be the ideal form of social product=
s. </p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px;=
 margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Zooming out, in a few decades, they’ll likely b=
e remembered as early experiments that subsequent generations of entreprene=
urs learned from to create better ones. But network effects are a hell of a=
 drug, and devilishly hard to overcome. Even if this generation of products=
 weren’t perfect, they were locked in. </p><p style=3D"color: #4=
04040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Whether thr=
ough incompetence or benevolence, Elon is breaking social’s shackles =
by killing what made Twitter just special enough to hang on. The world will=
 be better for it. Praise Elon.</p><p style=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 1=
6px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">…Or Twitter will find a =
way to cockroach itself back into our hearts and back onto the top of our S=
creen Time Reports, and I’ll have just wasted even more time talking =
about this beautiful, terrible, frustrating app for nothing. </p><p st=
yle=3D"color: #404040; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px=
